title: Supine
content_markdown:
#### Indications

- To maintain spinal alignment in patients with suspected or actual spinal injury.
- Diving emergencies.
- To improve venous return of blood to the heart in patients with low blood pressure.

#### Procedure

Figure 1

- Lower the head end of the stretcher by lifting the red leaver and pushing the head end in a downwards position until completely flat, then release the red leaver (see **Figure 1**).

- Remove any pillows.

- Have the patient position themselves in a supine position (if possible and if no spinal precautions). 
- In an unconscious or spinal immobilised patient, utilising two (or more, if necessary) assistants and appropriate lifting equipment, place the patient on the stretcher in a supine position.
- The lifting equipment must be removed prior to transport.
- To remove lifting equipment, a log roll can be performed (spine board), or the equipment disassembled (scoop stretcher).

#### Complications

- Vomiting leading to aspiration.
